title: potentilla heterophylla subsp. amphibola
intro: Potentilla heterophylla subsp. amphibola, commonly known as five-leaved potentilla or pink cinquefoil, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the rose family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. The health benefits of Potentilla heterophylla subsp. amphibola include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and support the immune system, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional medicine to create salves, creams, and ointments for topical application.
